question.content: For the cast-iron I-section beam with unequal top and bottom flange widths, use the beam geometry and loading given in the accompanying image. The second moment of area about the centroidal z-axis is \(I_z = 235 \times 10^6\,\text{mm}^4\), with \(y_1=119\,\text{mm}\) and \(y_2=181\,\text{mm}\). The allowable tensile stress is \([\sigma_t]=40\,\text{MPa}\), and the allowable compressive stress is \([\sigma_c]=120\,\text{MPa}\). Determine the allowable distributed load \([q]\), keeping two decimal places. Answer format: a single numerical value; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Use two base figures in sequence: the beam loading diagram and the unequal I-section diagram. For the beam model, write equilibrium for the whole beam: \(\sum M_A=0\Rightarrow 4F_{RB}-\frac{1}{2}(5q)4^2-8q=0\), and \(\sum F_y=0\Rightarrow F_{RA}+F_{RB}+4\cdot5q=0\). Solving gives \(F_{RA}=8q\) and \(F_{RB}=12q\).

step_4.content: From the bending-moment diagram, \(M_{\max}=6.4q\) and \(M_{\min}=-8q\). Apply the bending stress condition \(\sigma=\frac{My}{I_z}\le[\sigma]\) at the relevant fibers to obtain candidate allowable loads.
step_4.key_point: Use both positive and negative moment sections with different \(y_1, y_2\).

step_9.content: At section D: \([q_1]\le\frac{[\sigma_t]I_z}{6.4y_2}=\frac{40\times10^6\times235\times10^{-6}}{6.4\times0.181}=8.11\,\text{kN/m}\), and \([q_2]\le\frac{[\sigma_c]I_z}{6.4y_1}=\frac{120\times10^6\times235\times10^{-6}}{6.4\times0.119}=37.0\,\text{kN/m}\). On segment BC: \([q_3]\le\frac{[\sigma_t]I_z}{8y_1}=\frac{40\times10^6\times235\times10^{-6}}{8\times0.119}=9.87\,\text{kN/m}\), and \([q_4]\le\frac{[\sigma_c]I_z}{8y_2}=\frac{120\times10^6\times235\times10^{-6}}{8\times0.181}=195\,\text{kN/m}\). Therefore, \([q]=\min(q_1,q_2,q_3,q_4)=8.11\,\text{kN/m}\).
step_9.key_point: The governing constraint is \(q_1\), so the allowable load is 8.11 kN/m.
